context: Checkpoint 2026-04-30T12:04Z after pushing the large extensibility batch and adding two post-push Homestead activation hardening slices. Branch develop is clean and ahead of origin/develop by 4 local commits. Last remote push advanced origin/develop to fd7b9aa6 and GitHub Actions run 25163525166 completed successfully. Disk remains constrained but stable at about 7.4GB free; repo is about 1.6GB and node_modules about 975MB, so no cleanup was run because current artifacts are small relative to rebuild cost.
current_tasks: Remote checkpoint completed: pushed 51 local commits from develop to origin/develop (`3f512fae..fd7b9aa6`). GitHub Actions `Test & Quality` run 25163525166 completed successfully at head fd7b9aa6. CI noted only known non-blocking moderate audit findings: 9 moderate, 0 high, 0 critical.

Post-push local commits now ahead of origin/develop by 4: 583787e7 feat(homestead): reject unknown surface slots; 199003be docs(extensibility): document unknown surface slot rejection; 63400684 feat(homestead): reject unsupported surface kinds; 1bfe12e5 docs(extensibility): document surface kind allow-list.

Homestead surface activation now uses the shell's discovered DOM slot allow-list. Manifest-declared homestead surfaces targeting unknown slots are rejected as `unknown-slot` before wrappers are created; legacy `ui.slots` outside the current shell are ignored for compatibility.

Homestead now allow-lists surface `kind` values before activation. Defaults are `panel`, `widget`, `statusbar`, and `editor`; unsupported kinds are rejected as `unsupported-kind` until the host intentionally exposes behavior/trust semantics for that kind.

Prior completed path remains intact: manifest `extensions.surfaces`, Homestead surface mounts with DOM identity, `ui:surface_mounted` telemetry, rejected activation telemetry, SDK surface inspector helpers, Studio mounted/rejected diagnostics, opt-in stream demo plugin, DS primitive adoption for generic visuals, and Homestead stream rendering from `StreamSession`/`StreamChunk`.

Domain boundary remains: Homestead owns surface semantics, activation planning, stream rendering primitives, and diagnostic normalization; apps/dev owns Studio composition/experiments/diagnostic UI; @refarm.dev/ds owns only framework-agnostic visual primitives; BrowserSyncClient remains schema-neutral.

Validation passed after post-push slices: `npm --prefix packages/homestead test`, `npm --prefix packages/homestead run type-check`, `npm --prefix packages/homestead run build`, `npm --prefix apps/dev test`, `npm --prefix apps/dev run type-check`, `npm run agent:streaming:studio`, `npm run project:validate --silent`, and `git diff --check`.
blockers: Disk is still 99% used on the host mount with about 7.4GB free. The repo is about 1.6GB; current generated artifacts are not large enough to justify cleanup. Recheck disk before broad builds and clean only if free space falls near 2-3GB or new large artifacts appear.
Rust target dirs were previously removed by clean:heavy. Broad Rust checks will rebuild from scratch and may consume disk. Use scoped Rust commands from AGENTS.md only if Rust is touched.
Full tractor-ts unit suite can be noisy when WASM fixtures are missing after cleanup. Prefer focused tests or aggregate npm agent scripts unless intentionally rebuilding fixtures.
`apps/dev` importing from the Homestead SDK barrel can pull `Shell` and locale imports through dist. For inspector-only consumers use explicit subpath `@refarm.dev/homestead/sdk/surface-inspector`.
Pre-push hook lint/unit tests timed out locally during the large push but were non-blocking; CI passed. Audit moderate findings remain known and non-critical.
next_actions: First command after compaction: `git status --branch --short && df -h /workspaces/refarm && git log --oneline -12`.
If continuing local work before push, run economical touched-area gates: `npm run agent:streaming:studio && npm run project:validate --silent && git diff --check`.
Before next push, consider pushing the current 4 local commits and watching CI; the previous large batch already passed remote CI.

Next Homestead hardening slice: decide and implement where runtime trust should be enforced beyond manifest declaration and slot/kind/capability filtering. Candidate: annotate activation plan with trust profile/registry state, then emit a warning/rejection for unvalidated executable plugin surfaces while permitting internal Studio demo surfaces explicitly.
Next Studio diagnostics slice: optionally add a dedicated `/surfaces` Studio page if statusbar diagnostics become too cramped; keep the page in apps/dev and use Homestead inspector helpers.

Next extensibility slice: create a small executable multi-surface example package only if needed; examples should remain under `examples/` until executable code/build scripts justify a workspace package.
Next DS slice: only move genuinely agnostic visual styling into @refarm.dev/ds; do not move Homestead surface semantics or Studio diagnostics behavior into DS.
Run `npm run clean:light --silent` only at checkpoint boundaries if `.turbo`, dist, or validation artifacts grow meaningfully. Avoid heavy cleanup unless disk pressure is critical.
open_questions: Should `unknown-slot` rejection also apply to legacy `ui.slots`, or should legacy stay ignored for compatibility as implemented?
Which Homestead surface kinds should be accepted by default long-term? Current allow-list is `panel`, `widget`, `statusbar`, `editor`.
Where exactly should deeper runtime trust be enforced: before DOM wrapper creation in Homestead, inside PluginHost activation, or both with different responsibilities?
Should the Studio surface inspector become a `/surfaces` page, remain statusbar diagnostics, or both?
Should `ui.slots` be formally deprecated after one executable plugin-provided Homestead surface proves the new model?
key_decisions_pending: Runtime trust boundary for manifest-declared Homestead surfaces beyond slot/kind/capability checks.
Whether to push the 4 post-push local commits now or after one more small hardening slice.
First real executable multi-surface plugin example location: `examples/` vs workspace package.
Whether daily-driver parity checklist should be converted into canonical .project tasks after current surface path stabilizes.
